context: Almost every transaction on a trip ends with a price. You don't need to do maths in Spanish - you need to ask the price, recognise "euros", and catch the number that comes back. That's enough to pay confidently at any till or counter.
explanation: Lead with the question itself: "cuanto es?" (how much is it?). The answer comes in "euros", and if you spot a tag you can read "el precio" (the price). Master this trio and you can settle up anywhere before you even know the numbers.

examples.note: Your go-to question at any counter.
"Son" + a number is the typical reply.
